source: Isaac Newton — Opticks, Book I and III (Public Domain, Gutenberg 33504). Also: general scientific context on quantum mechanics.

lenses_puzzle.note: The correct order is Moon → Mercury → Sun (inverse of apparent hierarchy). This reflects the Copernican inversion: the apparent primacy of the Sun is a perceptual error.
lenses_puzzle.newton_fragment: Are not the rays of light very small bodies emitted from shining substances? And do not the several sorts of rays make vibrations of several bignesses?
lenses_puzzle.hint_text: The most apparent order is the wrong one. Begin with the smallest light.

heisenberg_principle.formal: The more precisely the position of some particle is determined, the less precisely its momentum can be known, and vice versa.
heisenberg_principle.poetic: The act of looking disturbs the looked-at. This has been proven. Uncertainty is not ignorance — it is a form of precision.
heisenberg_principle.puzzle_application: Walking blindfolded removes the observer. Without observation, the position is free to be anywhere. This is not a failure of knowledge — it is knowledge of a different kind.

quantum_fluctuation.description: Even in a perfect vacuum, quantum mechanics predicts fluctuations in the electromagnetic field. The vacuum is not empty — it seethes with virtual particles.
quantum_fluctuation.puzzle_application: Seven turns of waiting. The void is not empty. It measures itself.
quantum_fluctuation.proustian_bridge: The fluctuation reading triggers a flash — not of light but of time. The campanili di Martinville: a light observed from a moving vehicle that becomes, in the writing of it, something permanent.

natural_units.concept: In natural units (Planck units), fundamental constants including the speed of light (c), Planck's constant (ħ), and others are set equal to 1. This is not an approximation but a choice of measurement scale.
natural_units.implication: When you stop measuring in human units, all constants equal 1. Not because they are the same, but because measurement is always a comparison — and the only honest comparison is with the thing itself.

calibration.concept: All measurement flows from a chosen origin. There is no absolute zero in space — only the zero we agree upon.
calibration.note: The honest instrument knows its origin is chosen, not given.

the_constant_description: A prism of tangible light. It is warm. It refracts you. It measures nothing — it simply is the value that everything else reduces to.

llm_prompts.observatory_weight_0: The observatory is mathematically precise. Every observation collapses a probability. The protagonist is the instrument.
llm_prompts.observatory_weight_3plus: The instruments are too heavy to read. The protagonist's presence disturbs every measurement. The uncertainty principle applies to states of mind.
